title: The Forgotten Museum
subtitle: Chapter 01 · The First Fractures
introduction: A night guard crosses a quiet museum corridor and rests his palm on a display case for one deliberate second.
description: The night guard is Arthur, the grandson of the museum's architect. The dinosaur fossil on display was his grandfather's favorite piece, dusted nightly for forty years.
observation: The guard touches mahogany, checks a brass watch, and leaves a familiar handprint on the glass.
reconstruction: Carry Arthur's brass watch, the wooden case frame, and the worn exhibition ticket.
attunements: The watch engraving matches the fossil plaque.
The ticket carries Arthur's grandfather's name.
The handprint marks a daily act of remembrance.
revelation:
What was wrong: the display glass warmed to Arthur's palm before his hand could touch it.

Why it mattered: Arthur's grandfather did not leave him an empty artifact. He left him an instrument of time, where Arthur's forty years of daily devotion had worn a groove directly through the passage of time.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not that. Look at the glass exhibit case.
anomaly_definition.success_text: You saw it. The handprint manifests before any skin makes contact.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE PRINT APPEARS IN REVERSE.
capture_window.success_text: Timeline isolated: handprint precedes hand placement.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE WATCH IS TICKING EXACTLY ONE SECOND AHEAD. Inscription: 'To Arthur, keep the watch. Time is only a display case.'
MAHOGANY WOOD FRAME SHOWS FRICTION WARMTH. Arthur's touch leaves a permanent thermal footprint that defies thermodynamics.
Arthur's first entrance ticket from forty years ago, collected alongside thousands of others.
evidence_nodes.truth_connection: A pattern of memory is recorded.
